name: Halona Blowhole Lookout
tagline: A natural lava tube that shoots ocean spray 30 feet in the air — quick roadside stop with views of the rugged east coast.
parking: Free pullout lot on Kalanianaole Hwy. Small and fills fast midday
bestTime: Best spray during high surf and high tide — winter swells produce the most dramatic blasts. Morning light is best for photos
talkStory: This lava tube was formed by volcanic activity thousands of years ago and became internationally famous as the backdrop for the beach scene in 'From Here to Eternity' (1953) with Burt Lancaster and Deborah Kerr. The cove below the lookout is still called 'Eternity Beach' by locals. Multiple people have died climbing down to the blowhole or getting swept by rogue waves -- stay behind the guardrail. Winter swells produce the most dramatic 30-foot blasts.
